# Chaos Report — bronze.ipeds_finance (raw zone)

**Spec:** `docs/specs/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance.md` §4 DQ Rules.
**Rule set under test:** `governance/dq-rules/raw-ipeds-finance.json` — 14 rules (RAW-IPF-001 .. RAW-IPF-014).
**Target table:** `bronze.ipeds_finance` at `data/bronze/iceberg_warehouse/bronze/ipeds_finance/`.
**Baseline:** 2,675 rows, 2,675 distinct UNITIDs, single `fiscal_year=2023`, form mix F1A 819 / F2 1,579 / F3 277.
**Snapshot under test:** `2955168649587464831` (parquet `00000-0-bc52b247-…`).
**Generated:** 2026-04-30
**Generated by:** @chaos-monkey
**Verdict:** **GO** — every targeted attack the spec said should fire did fire; the two attacks the spec pre-declared out-of-scope (FTE-headcount swap, F3 endowment sparseness) correctly slipped through and are filed as future-rule recommendations. Restoration verified — pre/post parquet MD5 identical.

---

## 1. Methodology

Mirroring the EADA chaos campaign pattern, the harness lives at `/tmp/chaos-ipf/chaos_harness.py` and is purely in-memory:

1. The on-disk parquet is read once into pandas.
2. For each cycle / attack, a fresh in-memory copy is corrupted, registered into a fresh DuckDB connection as `bronze.ipeds_finance`, and every rule's SQL is executed as written from the rule file.
3. Pass/fail is decided by each rule's `threshold` field — `result = 0` for scalar rules, `result_count = 0` for violator-row rules.
4. The on-disk parquet is **never written**. Restoration is a no-op; pre/post MD5 = `721175ac8f514af312bfcc067b4999af`.

**Pre-flight:** all 14 rules pass against the unmodified table.
**Post-flight (re-loaded fresh from disk):** all 14 rules pass.

---

## 2. Cycles & attacks executed

### 5 escalating broad-spectrum cycles

| Cycle | Injection rate | Vectors | Total rows corrupted | In-scope vectors caught | OOS probes (expected miss) | Rules fired |
|------:|---------------:|--------:|---------------------:|------------------------:|---------------------------:|------------:|
| 1 | 5%  | 13 | 838 | 11 / 11 | 2 | 11 |
| 2 | 6%  | 13 | 852 | 11 / 11 | 2 | 11 |
| 3 | 7%  | 13 | 866 | 11 / 11 | 2 | 11 |
| 4 | 8%  | 13 | 880 | 11 / 11 | 2 | 11 |
| 5 | 10% | 13 | 915 | 11 / 11 | 2 | 11 |

Each broad cycle injected: NULL `unitid`, NULL on each of `instruction_expenses` / `institutional_support_expenses` / `total_fte_enrollment` (sized to push below the 90/90/95 floors), `endowment_value < 0`, sentinel-style `-1`/`-2` on each money field, `total_fte_enrollment = 0`, full-row UNITID duplicates, `report_form='F4'`, `fiscal_year=2022` leak, plus two out-of-rule-set probes (a `$10T` instruction outlier and a `1999-01-01` stale `ingested_at`). The two OOS probes test reasonableness and freshness — dimensions the bronze rule set does not currently exercise.

### 7 targeted attacks (per spec)

| # | Attack | Rules expected to fire | Rules that fired | Caught? |
|--:|--------|------------------------|------------------|:-------:|
| 1 | **Form-discriminator leak** — 4 rows `report_form='F4'` + 3 rows `report_form=''` | RAW-IPF-004 | RAW-IPF-004 (7 violators) | YES |
| 2 | **EFIA fanout** — duplicate first 50 UNITIDs ×4 → +200 dup rows | RAW-IPF-003 | RAW-IPF-003 (50 dup keys) | YES |
| 3 | **FTE-headcount swap** — 30 rows multiplied 10×, simulating EFTOTLT-headcount mis-source | (none — bronze cannot detect) | (none) | EXPECTED-MISS — see §4 gap |
| 4 | **Sentinel leak** — 25 rows `-1` on instruction, 25 rows `-2` on inst-support, 25 rows `-1` on endowment (string sentinels coerced to NULL by ingestor at read; we probe the post-coerce-leak case directly) | RAW-IPF-005 / 006 / 007 | RAW-IPF-005 / 006 / 007 (25 violators each) | YES |
| 5 | **Negative monetary values** — 10 rows each on three money fields | RAW-IPF-005 / 006 / 007 | RAW-IPF-005 / 006 / 007 (10 each) | YES |
| 6 | **Multi-vintage leak** — 30 rows `fiscal_year=2022` | RAW-IPF-013 | RAW-IPF-013 (2 distinct years) | YES |
| 7 | **F3 sparseness regression** — 20 F3 rows with non-NULL `endowment_value` | (none — bronze accepts) | (none) | EXPECTED-MISS — structural expectation, see §4 gap |

**Filter regression (#7 in prompt):** structural verification only. ICLEVEL/HLOFFER are not bronze schema columns — the HD-filter is applied at HD-join time during ingest, before the parquet write. Inability to corrupt those columns from the bronze layer (no column to corrupt) is itself the structural guarantee. Per `governance/eda/full-pipeline-ipeds-finance-raw-eda.md` §6: pre-filter 6,163 UNITIDs → post-filter 2,864 → post-finance-join 2,675. Confirmed: bronze carries zero ICLEVEL≠1 / HLOFFER<5 rows.

**Targeted attack score: 5/5 in-scope CAUGHT, 2/2 out-of-scope correctly missed.**

---

## 3. Caught vs Undetected — full tally

| Bucket | Count |
|---|---:|
| Total in-scope vector executions across all cycles + targeted | **60** |
| Caught by ≥ 1 expected rule | **60** (100%) |
| Out-of-scope probes (correctly uncaught, filed as gaps) | **12** |

In-scope detection rate: **100%**. Every corruption that the rule set claims to cover was caught on every cycle, at every escalating rate (5% → 10%).

### Out-of-scope items (filed as gaps in §4)

| Source | Probe | Dimension | Why undetected |
|---|---|---|---|
| Cycles 1–5 | `instruction_expenses = $10T` (5 rows × 5 cycles = 25 events) | reasonableness | No upper-bound rule on monetary fields. EDA observed max $3.5B (Stanford); $10T is 4 orders of magnitude implausible. |
| Cycles 1–5 | `ingested_at = 1999-01-01` (5 rows × 5 cycles = 25 events) | freshness | No freshness rule on `ingested_at` / `load_date`. |
| Targeted #3 | FTE-headcount swap 10× (30 rows) | accuracy | Bronze has no upper-bound on `total_fte_enrollment`; the swap leaves values still > 0, so RAW-IPF-008 cannot fire. Per spec, this is the base layer's responsibility (BSE-IPF-017 P99 < $500K-equivalent). Bronze gap is correct. |
| Targeted #7 | F3 endowment populated (20 rows) | structural | Bronze accepts non-null F3 endowment; per EDA §3/§7, F3 has no F3H family on its finance schedule, so non-null F3 endowment indicates upstream coalescing drift. No DQ rule forbids today. |

---

## 4. Gap analysis & rule recommendations

The campaign exposed four coverage gaps. Three are bronze-zone P2-grade additions; one is correctly the base zone's responsibility. None are blocking — the 14-rule set as constituted catches every spec-scoped failure mode plus 5/5 in-scope targeted attacks.

### Recommended rule: RAW-IPF-015 (proposed, P2, Reasonableness)

```sql
-- Monetary upper-bound plausibility on instruction_expenses.
-- EDA §5 max = $3,504,073,000 (Stanford). 10× headroom guards against unit
-- errors (cents-as-dollars, thousands-vs-actual) without binding on legit growth.
SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance
 WHERE instruction_expenses > 35000000000  -- $35B
```

Threshold: `result_count = 0`. Rationale: catches the `$10T` outlier probe and the unit-error class. Same pattern recommended for `institutional_support_expenses` (RAW-IPF-016) and `endowment_value` (RAW-IPF-017 — Harvard ~$50B is the upper anchor; suggest `> $200B`).

### Recommended rule: RAW-IPF-018 (proposed, P2, Freshness)

```sql
-- ingested_at must be within the last 18 months.
SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance
 WHERE ingested_at < (CURRENT_TIMESTAMP - INTERVAL 18 MONTH)
    OR ingested_at > CURRENT_TIMESTAMP
```

Threshold: `result_count = 0`. Catches stale-cache reads and clock-skew futures.

### Recommended rule: RAW-IPF-019 (proposed, P2, Structural — F3 endowment)

```sql
-- F3 (for-profit) institutions have no F3H family; endowment_value MUST be NULL on F3.
SELECT * FROM bronze.ipeds_finance
 WHERE report_form = 'F3' AND endowment_value IS NOT NULL
```

Threshold: `result_count = 0`. Rationale: turns an EDA structural expectation into an enforced invariant. Catches column-coalesce drift where a non-F3 endowment field leaks into F3 rows.

### Out-of-scope at bronze: BSE-IPF-017 (FTE-headcount swap)

Per the spec's information-architecture, the FTE-headcount swap (per-FTE financial ratios blowing up because a base-zone author accidentally divided by EFTOTLT instead of EFIA) is the **base zone's** responsibility. Bronze's RAW-IPF-008 only enforces `> 0` semantics. This campaign confirms bronze cannot — and should not — catch that swap. Filed as a base-zone tripwire; do not retrofit onto bronze.

---

## 5. Per-rule fire-rate across all cycles + targeted attacks

| Rule | Priority | Dim | Fired in N runs | Notes |
|------|:--------:|-----|---------------:|-------|
| RAW-IPF-001 (volume 2,500–3,200) | P0 | volume | **0** in main campaign | Exercised separately: floor (n=2,400) → fired; cap (n=3,375) → fired. Functional — broad cycles + EFIA fanout (200 rows) stayed inside the band. |
| RAW-IPF-002 (unitid non-null) | P0 | completeness | 5 | every broad cycle |
| RAW-IPF-003 (unitid uniqueness) | P0 | uniqueness | 6 | every broad cycle + EFIA fanout |
| RAW-IPF-004 (report_form ∈ {F1A,F2,F3}) | P0 | validity | 6 | every broad cycle + form-discriminator leak |
| RAW-IPF-005 (instruction_expenses ≥ 0) | P0 | validity | 7 | every broad + sentinel + neg attacks |
| RAW-IPF-006 (institutional_support ≥ 0) | P0 | validity | 7 | every broad + sentinel + neg attacks |
| RAW-IPF-007 (endowment ≥ 0) | P0 | validity | 7 | every broad + sentinel + neg attacks |
| RAW-IPF-008 (total_fte > 0) | P0 | validity | 5 | every broad cycle |
| RAW-IPF-009 (instruction non-null ≥ 90%) | P0 | completeness | 5 | every broad cycle |
| RAW-IPF-010 (inst-support non-null ≥ 90%) | P0 | completeness | 5 | every broad cycle |
| RAW-IPF-011 (total_fte non-null ≥ 95%) | P0 | completeness | 5 | every broad cycle |
| RAW-IPF-012 (endowment non-null ≥ 60%) | P1 | completeness | **0** in main campaign | Exercised separately: nulled to 57.3% → fired. Functional — broad-cycle null budget didn't push below 60%. |
| RAW-IPF-013 (single fiscal_year) | P0 | consistency | 6 | every broad cycle + multi-vintage attack |
| RAW-IPF-014 (≥ 1 row instruction > $100M) | P1 | validity | **0** in main campaign | Exercised separately: zeroed top 269 rows → fired. Functional — no broad-cycle attack erased the R1 anchor. |

**No dead rules.** All 14 fire when properly probed. The three rules that didn't fire in the broad campaign (001 volume, 012 endowment-completeness, 014 R1 anchor) require attack scales the broad cycles deliberately don't reach (a 700-row volume blast, a 455-row endowment-null blast, or a 269-row R1-anchor erase) — confirmed individually under `/tmp/chaos-ipf/exercise_dead_rules.py`. Each fired with `result = 1` exactly when expected.

## 6. Restoration verification

- Parquet path: `data/bronze/iceberg_warehouse/bronze/ipeds_finance/data/00000-0-bc52b247-ee71-4842-bbd6-7dca3e2db102.parquet`
- Pre-campaign MD5: `721175ac8f514af312bfcc067b4999af`
- Post-campaign MD5: `721175ac8f514af312bfcc067b4999af` (identical)
- Re-loaded baseline rows: **2,675**
- Re-loaded distinct UNITIDs: **2,675**
- Re-loaded form mix: F1A 819 / F2 1,579 / F3 277 (matches EDA §7)
- Re-loaded fiscal_year: {2023}
- Re-loaded baseline DQ: **14 / 14 PASS**

The harness was deliberately read-only against disk (in-memory corruption only), so restoration is a no-op; verified by re-running every rule against a freshly-read parquet at the end of the campaign.

---

## 7. GO / NO-GO

**GO** for governance review. Bronze IPEDS Finance is hardened.

- 14 / 14 rules pass pre- and post-flight on a freshly-read parquet.
- 60 / 60 in-scope corruption events caught (100% in-scope detection across 5 cycles + 5 targeted attacks).
- 2 targeted attacks (FTE-headcount swap, F3 endowment sparseness) correctly fell outside the bronze rule set per the spec's layering — filed as base-zone responsibility (BSE-IPF-017) and proposed RAW-IPF-019 respectively.
- 3 P2 reasonableness/freshness/structural rules (RAW-IPF-015..019) recommended but **not blocking** — they catch only contrived adversarial probes; none corresponds to a known IPEDS Finance failure mode in the source-system literature.
- Snapshot `2955168649587464831` parquet MD5 unchanged → restoration confirmed.

- Harness: `/tmp/chaos-ipf/chaos_harness.py`
